metadata.reliability_note: Map produced from pasted English text (NIV). Hebrew was not consulted. For STA Studio testing only.

classification.local_register_shifts: v.19-20 carry an INTIMATE / family undertone (foster-father instruction, continued obedience). v.21-23 sit firmly in court-official register.
classification.suggested_pace: steady, with a slight quickening from discovery to record

level_1.prose_arc: The passage moves from a quiet positioning shot into a sudden danger and its swift resolution. The arc traces continued concealment, hidden loyalty, threat to the king, faithful disclosure, public justice, and official record. The burden is the unrecognized faithfulness of two hidden figures whose very hiddenness is what makes the king's rescue possible. The concern is whether loyalty kept in the dark will reach the throne in time and whether it will be remembered.
level_1.context: The setting is the Persian royal court under King Xerxes. Esther is queen, but her Jewish identity remains hidden by her foster-father's earlier command. Mordecai sits at the king's gate, a position of administrative access close to power, while two royal doorkeepers guard the king's threshold. The reader needs to know that Esther's secrecy is obedience rather than deception, and that Mordecai's saving service here will become structurally important much later in the book.
level_1.emotion_tone: The dominant tone is restrained and watchful. The opening is quiet, almost domestic, in its memory of a daughter still listening to her foster-father. The middle turns sharp with hidden anger and treachery, and the disclosure is urgent but contained. The closing is procedural, with weight gathering around what is not said aloud — Esther's hidden identity, Mordecai's unrewarded loyalty, the king's narrow escape — rather than around any expressed emotion.
level_1.communicative_function: The passage transmits a community memory and plants a debt that the larger narrative will later require the king to repay. It records a saved life, names the source of that saving, and enters the event into royal memory. It also shows hidden faithfulness operating inside an empire that does not know it is being served. Speaking the passage rehearses providential timing: a foster-father's silent obedience, a queen's quiet voice, and a chronicle that does not yet know its own importance.

concept_bank.note: Administrative and judicial position; not merely a doorway. Needs controlled rendering so the listener understands Mordecai is in a place of access, not idle.
A court custom whose internal logic is not explained; will need a controlled handling that does not invent reasons.

concept_bank.term: Family background and nationality (her people and kindred)
concept_bank.note: Esther's Jewish identity; the very thing concealed; carries covenantal weight that the passage does not name aloud.

concept_bank.term: King's officers / eunuchs guarding the doorway
concept_bank.note: Persian palace role; some translations render as 'eunuchs', others as 'officers'; needs target-language controlled rendering.

concept_bank.term: Conspire to lay hand on the king
concept_bank.note: Biblical idiom in many sources for assassination; this rendering says 'assassinate' directly. May need flagging if the source-language form is more idiomatic.

concept_bank.term: Impaling on poles / hanging on a tree
concept_bank.note: Persian execution; the image and its meaning both need controlled handling.
Institution of royal memory; this is the same record that becomes structurally important in Esther 6.

scenes.title: Mordecai at the gate, Esther's continued obedience

people.name: Mordecai
people.relationship: Esther's adoptive guardian and cousin, Jew within Persian court
people.wants: to remain near power and watch over Esther

people.name: Esther
people.role: queen who continues to obey her foster-father
people.relationship: queen of Persia, Mordecai's adopted daughter and cousin
people.wants: to keep her identity hidden as instructed
people.carries: hidden Jewish identity, divided loyalty between palace and family

people.name: The virgins
people.relationship: candidates from the earlier court process, now reassembled
people.carries: presence that frames the timing of the scene

people.name: King Xerxes (referenced, not present)
people.role: ruler in whose court the gathering takes place
people.carries: imperial authority that shapes the conditions of secrecy

places.name: King's gate
places.meaning: place of access, judgment, and royal business
places.effect_on_scene: places Mordecai near information and protection

places.name: Royal court (implied)
places.role: setting for the assembly and for Esther's life
places.effect_on_scene: holds the conditions of secrecy that shape Esther's obedience

objects.name: Second gathering of virgins
objects.what_it_is: a second assembly of young women at court
objects.signals: continuation of a court procedure already established earlier in the book

objects.name: Mordecai's earlier instruction (referenced)
objects.signals: continuity of authority from upbringing into queenship

objects.name: Esther's upbringing under Mordecai (referenced)
objects.what_it_is: the period when Mordecai raised her
objects.function_in_scene: precedent that frames her present obedience
objects.signals: that her present silence is part of a long-formed habit

scenes.what_happens: Mordecai sits at the king's gate while Esther continues to keep her family and people hidden, as Mordecai had instructed.
scenes.significant_absence: No one in the court knows Esther is Jewish.

scenes.title: The conspiracy

people.name: Mordecai
people.relationship: kinsman of the queen, Jew unknown to court
people.carries: hidden identity, hearing distance to court business

people.name: Bigthana
people.relationship: king's officer with access to the king's threshold

people.name: Teresh
people.relationship: king's officer with access to the king's threshold

people.name: King Xerxes
people.carries: imperial life made vulnerable through trusted servants

places.name: King's gate
places.effect_on_scene: enables Mordecai to overhear plotted treason

places.name: Doorway (referenced)
places.type: protected entry to the king's quarters
places.meaning: point of access whose betrayal is most dangerous
places.effect_on_scene: explains how the conspirators would have opportunity

objects.name: Anger of the two officers
objects.what_it_is: an unstated grievance against the king
objects.signals: that loyalty inside the palace is breakable

objects.name: Conspiracy / plot
objects.what_it_is: a secret plan to kill the king
objects.function_in_scene: the threat the passage turns on

scenes.what_happens: Two royal doorkeepers, angered with the king, plot together to assassinate him.
scenes.communicative_purpose: Introduces hidden treason against the king.

scenes.title: Discovery and disclosure

people.name: Mordecai
people.wants: to protect the king through the queen
people.carries: knowledge that must be moved upward

people.name: Esther
people.role: messenger from Mordecai to the king
people.wants: to relay the warning faithfully and credit its source

people.name: King Xerxes
people.wants: to know of threats against him

places.name: King's gate (implied)
places.effect_on_scene: continues to function as listening post

places.name: Palace interior (implied)
places.role: where Esther speaks to the king
places.meaning: place of intimate access to the throne

objects.name: Knowledge of the plot
objects.function_in_scene: content moved through a chain of disclosure
objects.signals: the path from gate to queen to king

objects.name: Credit given to Mordecai
objects.what_it_is: Esther's naming of Mordecai as the source
objects.function_in_scene: links the saving information to a specific person
objects.signals: that this loyalty is on record from this moment

scenes.what_happens: Mordecai learns of the plot and reports it through Esther, who tells the king and names Mordecai as the source.
scenes.communicative_purpose: Warning reaches the king through faithful disclosure.

scenes.title: Investigation, execution, record

people.name: King Xerxes
people.role: authority who orders investigation and punishment
people.wants: to verify and answer the threat

people.name: Investigators (implied, unnamed)
people.wants: role-relative — to determine truth of the report

people.name: Royal scribe / record-keeper (implied)
people.role: the one who writes the event into the annals
people.wants: role-relative — to enter the event into royal memory

people.name: Mordecai (referenced, not present)
people.carries: loyalty now entered into the record but not yet rewarded

places.name: Royal court (implied)
places.role: place where the annals are kept and read
places.effect_on_scene: enables the recording to occur in the king's presence

places.name: Book of the annals
places.effect_on_scene: preserves the saved-life event for later remembrance

objects.name: Investigation
objects.what_it_is: a check of the report's truth

objects.name: Book of the annals
objects.signals: memory that may later be consulted

objects.name: Presence of the king at the recording
objects.what_it_is: the king's witnessing of the entry
objects.function_in_scene: anchors the recording inside royal authority
objects.signals: that the saved-life event is part of royal record

objects.name: Reward for Mordecai (absent)
objects.what_it_is: an honor or reward expected after such service
objects.signals: a debt left unpaid that the larger narrative will later remember

scenes.what_happens: The report is investigated and confirmed; the two officials are impaled, and the event is recorded in the royal annals in the king's presence.
scenes.communicative_purpose: Records the saved-life event in royal memory.
scenes.significant_absence: No reward is given to Mordecai.
